title: HTTP/1.1 Response Serialization, Field Order, Framing, and Writes · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs http/1.1 response serialization, field order, framing, and writes from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the canonical bounded HTTP/1.1 response serializer. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for canonical bounded HTTP/1.1 response serializer after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Commit one status line, validated field section, empty line, framed content, and trailers in protocol order.
Reject CR, LF, NUL, controls, invalid names, and unsafe values before serializing any response field.
Choose no-content, fixed-length, chunked, or close-delimited output from method, status, known length, streaming, version, and reuse policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Response Commit Order · changed case · Review Response Commit Order
steps.instruction:
review case — A review protocol team handles case 7607 in a signed webhook receiver behind an HTTP/2 to HTTP/1.1 gateway. Build deterministic Go evidence to select one output framing mode while retaining partial-write and field-validation evidence; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then inject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and close before any downstream forwarding. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Commit one status line, validated field section, empty line, framed content, and trailers in protocol order.

Which action demonstrates Response Commit Order without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Response Commit Order · changed case · Review Response Commit Order.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for commit one status line, validated field section, empty line, framed content, and trailers in protocol order. For Response Commit Order · changed case · Review Response Commit Order, keep Response Commit Order connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
